note: The Stage 1 proving run, 18 Jul 2026, $0.000573. The judge returned PASS and cited the raise. The all-satisfied control: a dataset made only of broken code rewards a judge that blocks everything, and this is one of the three cases that stops it.

labels.text: truncate rejects a negative length with a clear error
labels.why: an explicit guard raises ValueError before slicing, and the message names the offending value.

labels.text: the result never exceeds length characters
labels.why: text[:length] is bounded by length, and the negative case is already refused above.
